mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS and declining UNRATE occur but 20-day SPX returns are negative in >40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer demand, which correlates with higher corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Concurrent HOUST and RSAFS growth occur but 5-day SPX returns are negative in >30% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening supply-side capacity, which increases inflationary pressures and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are positive in >50% of instances despite INDPRO declines exceeding 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank hawkishness, leading to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances despite CPIAUCSL above 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDP growth (GDPC1) during a monetary tightening cycle indicates economic slowdown, which correlates with negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are positive in >50% of instances despite FEDFUNDS >1.5% and GDPC1 <1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances despite UNRATE increases exceeding 0.3pp.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production (INDPRO) during low inflation (CPIAUCSL < 2%) indicates robust supply-side growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns are negative in >40% of instances despite INDPRO >104 and CPIAUCSL <2%.
